cases.input: Claude flagged three issues in our auth diff; can you turn that into a proper AI-assisted review report with file-line evidence?
Here is a staged diff on src/billing/invoice.ts — review it for correctness and tests and give me a merge decision.
Our RAG summarizer has no gold labels — score how grounded its summaries are against the retrieved chunks and report a baseline.
A 900-word essay was submitted with no watermark; tell us the likelihood it was AI-generated without accusing the student.
Define the full testing strategy for our fraud-scoring API across model, data-quality, fairness, and CI/CD layers.
Propose pytest cases plus a bounded fuzz spec for pricing/discount.py, and tell me which generated assertions are tautological.
Build a safety packet for a customer-support chatbot that must refuse medical-advice prompts and escalate to a human owner.
Generate API reference docs for our handlers from openapi.yaml and flag any endpoints the spec describes but the code does not implement.
Design an LLM-in-the-loop support-triage workflow with a human approval gate before any auto-reply is sent.
Is this generated marketing blurb good enough to ship, and is it even AI-written?
Review this model output for hallucinations and also write the unit tests for the parser in the same answer.
Our safety validator passed — can you confirm the chatbot is safe to launch to all users?
